Zonal isolation/casing pressure repair. 
. A cement retainer was set at 15,045' MD.
. The perforated interval(s) below were squeezed w/ ~16 ppg Class H cement to a squeeze pressure of 2,900 psi.
Squeeze DS-1 (isolated-15,072'-15,204' MD); DS-1.5 (open-15,158'-15,200' MD); DS-2 (open-15,224'-15,260') and DS-3 (open-15,280'-15,330') perforations on 1/24/21.
. 100' of cement was then placed on top of the retainer, back to a TOC depth of 14,945' MD.
. Obtained a successful negative test, pressure testing of the plug. 
. The wellbore was left with a full column of 10.4 ppg Calcium Chloride Brine that will provide an overbalanced hydrostatic barrier on top of the cement plugs.
Met 30 CFR 250.1715 (a)(3)(iii)(A) requirements by placement of the cement retainer, squeezing of the producing interval(s), and placing 100' of cement on top of the retainer. Plugs tested in accordance to the regulation and the approved APM. Tested cement plug to 1,137 psi utilizing OTC Greenlight on 1/27/21, good test. 
Cement was not placed outside of the tubing, on top of the production packer, at this time. (Potential plans for removal of the production tubing and recompleting the well.)
